chunks.content:
Article: Rei Kaworu connection
[]: Rei/Kaworu connection             Quote Post by Staffy3 » Mon Nov 29, 2010 4:18 pm Ok, we all know that Kaworu and Rei have red eyes and are perceived by viewers to be the Adam and Lilith of the "modern age." Well, what about the way Kaworu addresses Rei in the Director's cut of episode 24? "We are the same." Certainly it could be dismissed that they are similar in that they are the reincarnarion of Adam/Lilith, Angels etc. but what about their Surnames? Nagisa and Ayanami. Not only do the words refer to Sea and Shore, a reference to the ending of EoE, but also Izanami and Izanagi are the Adam of Lilith of the Shinto religion. I thought this to be interesting considering the majority of references are related with Christianity and Judaism. I'm sorry if this or anything similar has been posted before. I'd love to hear your opinions on this. :)

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Nov 29, 2010 4:28 pm Under the judeochristian garb Anno smuggles in a lot of buddhist and shinto thought. No surprises there Albeit how is Ayanami's name related to Sea? My Japanese is too much fail to know that

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Nov 29, 2010 4:34 pm Rei : Vessel for the soul of Lilith : Genetic lineage of Yui Ikari Kaworu : Vessel for the soul of Adam : Genetic lineage Indeterminate All laid out pretty well. The wiki has more information on this: http://wiki.evageeks.org/Rei_Ayanami http://wiki.evageeks.org/Kaworu_Nagisa

[]:             Quote Post by Staffy3 » Mon Nov 29, 2010 4:35 pm Ayanami (綾波) literally means "twill wave", the Kanji meaning "twill" and "wave". Taken from the EvaWiki, I believe Nagisa means beach. Nagisa can be written using different kanji characters and can mean: 渚, "beach/shore" 汀, "water's edge/shore" 凪砂, "lull, sand" Hope this clears things up for you. @

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Mon Nov 29, 2010 8:37 pm Even their appearances relate to the wave/ocean image: it's quite likely that Rei was intentionally given blue hair because of the connection with water. Also, I can't help but think that Rei and Kaworu are innately enemies. The fact that they are opposing progenitor beings is enough, but the look on Rei's face in Ep. 24 (right after she projects an ATF equal to Kaworu's) looks outright hostile...then again, it could just be

[]:             Quote Post by Staffy3 » Tue Nov 30, 2010 3:49 am Their names are a problem to that particular theory, but what about Eva unit 01 being called an "Oni"? I'm sure that was intentional, surely there are other beasts with apt descriptions they could have named it

Article: Rei Kaworu connection
0 3:49 am Their names are a problem to that particular theory, but what about Eva unit 01 being called an "Oni"? I'm sure that was intentional, surely there are other beasts with apt descriptions they could have named it after? Possibly Rei's personality could be similar to Kaworu's when she is Rei 1/3? Just another thought and possibly further evidence to back up my theory, but possibly unrelated coincidences knowing Anno's sneaky red herrings...

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Nov 30, 2010 3:54 am from http://www.evacommentary.org/appendix/character-names.html copied from Japan.Anime.Evangelion postings in December of 2000 This character was named by the screenplay writer Akio Satsukawa. Nagisa [shore] is a word related to the sea. Also the kanji nagisa 渚 consists of katakana SHI シ and kanji SHA 者, therefore he's SHISHA シ者 [messenger 使者]. He said it also comes from the movie director Nagisa Oshima. But what is Kaworu? Sorry, I will ask him next time.

[]:             Quote Post by Staffy3 » Tue Nov 30, 2010 3:58 am @Aura Twilight: Your idea that Kaworu is derived from Gendo brings up their meeting as an important factor. Yui as Rei feeling dissapointed that she was left to remain in the Eva, sacrificed by her husband. It also could show what Gendo could be like if he could show emotion toward Shinji, albeit over the top as in Ep. 24 infamous bath scene XD

[]:             Quote Post by TheLobe » Tue Nov 30, 2010 4:32 am Xard wrote: The biggest problem here is the fact Rei was named after warship and Kaworu's name *wasn't even Anno's decision* but made by screenwriter for ep 24. The meaning of shore was intentional, yes, but it's untenable stretch to think Kaworu was named Nagisa to play opposite to Rei. It's not downright impossible per se, but still unlikely. Shame, really. I rather like the theory

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Nov 30, 2010 11:37 am And on a completely different

Article: Rei Kaworu connection
 downright impossible per se, but still unlikely. Shame, really. I rather like the theory

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Nov 30, 2010 11:37 am And on a completely different note from that, in response to my earlier one-liner about mother-figures: Reichu wrote: Kaworu is associated with both mothers and fathers. I'll be the first to vouch for the mom thing (heck, for all I know I -was- the first; getting burned at the stake at ANF... GOOD TIMES!), but the Gendo connection is so blatant I'm sure you can spot all the relevant cues without my help. :3 What's more, Kaworu is thoroughly linked to Kaji, whom Shinji's been known to look up to as a male role model. Yeah, there are SOME links to Misato, but not enough exclusive ones to warrant the leaps required to justify her being the genetic donor. Kaworu looks a lot more like a Lilin than the one known parent -- Adam -- so we can probably guess that he bears a very close resemblance to the donor. Also, since Kaworu is ostensibly male, and Adam logically is not, the donor must be male (unless Misato has some chromosomal defect she hasn't told us about). The "Misato" thing just doesn't wash: wrong sex, no resemblance, and why would they use a kid for the donor anyway? I know Misato's age at the time (not-exactly-14) gets brought up a lot as "evidence" (14-year-old pilots and all), but this is pretty flimsy on its own. Misato's mere presence acting as "reasonable suspicion" (as cited in that Rei's Soul thread) doesn't really work, either. We have a concrete precedent in the show for "bring your kid to work and traumatize them for life day" with Yui and Shinji. Also, adult test subjects are used in both other contact experiments (both scientist-parents of main characters). (If there is any logical association between the eventual use of teenagers as pilots in Project E -- after failed synchro-whatever experiments using adults --, and the KatsExp's molestation of Adam in 2000, I'd like to hear it.) If you want to "pick a donor" based on neat thematic alignments, Dr. K is your best bet, to be perfectly honest. There are so many connections that people would

Article: Rei Kaworu connection
 of Adam in 2000, I'd like to hear it.) If you want to "pick a donor" based on neat thematic alignments, Dr. K is your best bet, to be perfectly honest. There are so many connections that people would be chocking it up to Anno's genius or something if only he'd included the minuscule additional amount of writing necessary to indicate that he gave even the slightest damn about who the donor was. (And it's a pity he didn't, since it would have followed up Misato's character development in #12 oh so nicely.)
